reviewer: new-demo build wave (headless Chrome, persistent-profile: real 444 MB download + real int8-WASM inference on index + all 4 ladder pages, cached auto-init across pages, canvas-non-blank + relight-interactivity probes, mobile+desktop overflow, desktop screenshots reviewed)

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser inference verified. Runnability was confirmed before building three ways: (1) Python onnxruntime on model_quantized.onnx — input pixel_values [1,3,1024,768], output predicted_normal [1,3,512,384]; center-pixel unit normal [-0.117,0.337,0.900] (mostly +Z toward camera for a front-facing portrait — a genuine normal field, mean normalized components ≈[−0.035,−0.029,0.986]). (2) In-browser ORT-web WASM harness (onnxruntime-web@1.21.0) — same dims [1,3,512,384], center normal [-0.074,0.331,0.941], matching Python. (3) Build verify (persistent profile, headless): fresh Download (444 MB) → ready → Estimate produced a non-blank RGB normal map in 88256 ms; ladder pages basics/practical/wild/multi-model each produced real output (≈88 s each). transformers.js@3.7.5 has NO `sapiens` model class, so the demo runs the ONNX graph directly via raw ONNX Runtime Web in a Web Worker (isolated per-worker pin, like yolov10-detection).
rubric.notes: Model onnx-community/sapiens-normal-0.3b (Sapiens human-centric ViT), int8 (q8) model_quantized.onnx.

rubric.evidence: Expected output shape + semantic sanity confirmed: [1,3,512,384] per-pixel XYZ vectors, L2-normalized in the worker, RGB-encoded as (n*0.5+0.5)*255 → the classic pastel normal map (camera-facing surfaces read light-blue). See-inside shows the exact shape, the mean normal direction, and a live pixel-pick vector read on hover. The relighting pages prove the field is semantically correct: Lambertian N·L shading produces geometrically-plausible highlights that track a moving light (verified the litCanvas changes and stays non-blank after an ArrowLeft light move).
rubric.notes: Distinct dense task from depth: per-pixel ORIENTATION, not distance. Relight/pixel-pick reuse the cached normal field — no re-inference.

rubric.evidence: conformance declares-model-id=pass (onnx-community/sapiens-normal-0.3b linked to its card), declares-task=pass (image-to-image), declares-backend=pass (WASM), declares-quantisation=pass (q8/int8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker + worker.js). At-a-glance names the exact model, runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21), input/output tensor shapes, int8 quant, and honestly records the CC-BY-NC-4.0 license (non-commercial, attribution; weights loaded unmodified from the canonical repo, not re-hosted).
rubric.notes: No invented API — the sapiens graph is run directly with hand-written ImageNet preprocessing + L2-normalize postprocessing.

rubric.evidence: conformance shared-auto-init-loader=pass. Persistent-profile cross-page probe: the index page first-visit showed Download → downloaded 444 MB → ready; then basics/practical/wild/multi-model each opened with loader state ALREADY 'ready' (auto-init from the shared Cache Storage), NO Download button present, run enabled — one 444 MB download served all five pages. The worker fetches model_quantized.onnx THROUGH Cache Storage (sapiens-normal-onnx-cache) so lib/model-cache.js scans '/onnx-community/sapiens-normal-0.3b/' and the clear-cache control + storage readout work (observed '444 MB cached of 11182 MB').
rubric.notes: Never silently re-downloads the large model; every page uses createModelLoader.

rubric.evidence: conformance loads-clean=pass (0 console errors/exceptions on load across all 5 pages), live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page=pass. A prominent warn-box sets the honest ~60–90 s latency + 444 MB size expectation up front; the run status announces the long-estimate expectation; download progress streams from the worker into the loader bar; failures surface in .status.err + loader Retry. Fresh-profile load shows the honest Download state — no silent large download.
rubric.notes: requiresWebGPU:false — runs on the no-GPU headless runner (WASM), so there is no unsupported-device dead-end; the cost is honestly the WASM latency, which the page states.

rubric.evidence: Every control exercised: sample thumbs + dropzone/file (role=button, Enter/Space), Estimate button, the pixel-pick hover on the normal map, the practical/multi-model light-pad (role=slider, pointer drag AND arrow-key operable — verified ArrowLeft re-lit the surface), the ambient/fog sliders, and the wild three-light colour pickers + intensity sliders + preset buttons. buttons-have-names=pass, images-have-alt=pass.
rubric.notes: Interactive relighting operates on cached normals (no re-inference), coalesced via requestAnimationFrame.

rubric.evidence: All visible output — the normal map, the relit surface, the 2.5D fogged scene — is produced by the on-device run + deterministic shading of the real normal field. Canvas-non-blank probes with a non-uniformity check confirm genuine content, not a placeholder. The 88 s latency itself is evidence the model actually runs (no shortcut).

conformance images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass, live-status-region=pass. Sample images have descriptive alt; the normal map, sphere legend, and relit canvases carry aria-labels; the light-pad is role=slider with aria-valuetext updated on move and full arrow-key operation; status is role=status aria-live=polite. Sample/dropzone are keyboard-operable.
rubric.notes: Sample-strip aria-label notes the portrait is public-domain and the faces synthetic.

rubric.evidence: conformance responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass. Measured horizontal overflow = 0 px at BOTH desktop 1280×800 and mobile 360×740 (DPR3) on all 5 pages. Layout uses flex:1 1 280px + min-inline-size:0 (css-layout guidance) so the input/output canvas cards and the light controls wrap instead of overflowing; canvases are inline-size:100% with aspect from width/height attrs. Desktop + mobile screenshots reviewed — legible, controls stack, no clipping.

Inference AND the dense RGB normal-map composite run in the Web Worker (encodeToBitmap on an OffscreenCanvas → transferred ImageBitmap), so the main thread only draws. Relighting is a per-pixel N·L pass at the model's native 384×512 (~196k px, ≈2–3 ms — under the 8 ms frame slice) coalesced with requestAnimationFrame, so dragging the light stays responsive. The honest performance cost is the ~88 s single-threaded-WASM inference (GitHub Pages can't set COOP/COEP for WASM threads); this is surfaced prominently and only blocks the worker, never the main-thread UI.
rubric.notes: A WebGPU build would be far faster; the universal WASM path is shipped for reach. Guidance: break-up-long-tasks / identify-inp-causes (heavy inference + dense composite off the main thread; relight coalesced per frame).

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel canvas grid no horizontal overflow on mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex:1 1 <basis> and min-inline-size:0 on items with wide content so they shrink instead of overflowing; prefer logical properties and aspect-ratio for media.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — input/output canvas cards use flex:1 1 280px with min-inline-size:0 and wrap on narrow viewports; canvases are inline-size:100%. Verified 0 px horizontal overflow at 360×740 and 1280×800 on all 5 pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work off main thread web worker plus coalesce per frame
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move long main-thread JavaScript off the main thread; don't run heavy loops during interactions; coalesce frequent updates.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — the ~88 s inference and the dense normal-map RGB composite run in a module worker (raw ORT-web, OffscreenCanvas → transferred ImageBitmap). Relighting runs on the returned cached normal field, per-pixel at native res, coalesced with requestAnimationFrame so light-drag never floods the main thread.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible slider light direction keyboard operable status live region
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give every control an accessible name + keyboard operation; announce async state in a role=status aria-live region; make custom sliders role=slider with aria-valuetext and arrow-key support.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — the custom light-pad is role=slider with aria-valuetext and arrow-key movement (verified re-lighting via keyboard), status is role=status aria-live=polite, and all buttons/thumbs/canvases are labelled.

openQuestions: The int8 WASM path is ~88 s/image. A WebGPU (ORT-web EP, fp16/q4f16) path would cut this to seconds — worth a progressive-enhancement follow-up once verifiable on a GPU runner (couldn't verify a GPU path headless here).
Sapiens is human-centric; a general-scene normal model (e.g. a browser-runnable DSINE/omnidata ONNX) would broaden the samples beyond people — none found runnable in transformers.js/ORT-web today.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a WebGPU (ORT-web WebGPU EP) fast path for Sapiens normals using the fp16/q4f16 build, gated on a real adapter probe with the verified WASM int8 path as the honest fallback.
Add an AR makeup/material try-on wild demo that overlays a coloured material and shades it by the normal field.

summary: New demo: on-device SURFACE-NORMAL estimation via Sapiens (onnx-community/sapiens-normal-0.3b, int8, ~444 MB) run through raw ONNX Runtime Web in a worker — a distinct dense-prediction task (per-pixel 3D orientation, not depth). Index + 4 ladder pages (basics normal map, practical relighting studio, wild three-point colour rig, multi-model normals+depth 2.5D relight), all verified with real in-browser inference, one-download cross-page cached auto-init, clean console, and mobile+desktop parity. Honest about the ~60–90 s single-threaded-WASM latency and the CC-BY-NC license.
